question: Which type of network diagram shows the actual cabling, rack positions, and physical connections between devices?
feedback: A physical diagram documents where equipment sits and how it is cabled. A logical diagram abstracts that away to show IP addressing, VLANs, and routing.

question: Which of the following are typically shown on a Layer 3 network diagram?
incorrect: The specific patch cable colors between switches
The exact rack unit where each device is mounted
The serial number printed on each transceiver
feedback: Layer 3 diagrams focus on IP addressing, subnets, and routing. Cabling and rack positions belong on physical/rack diagrams.

question: Captain Doran is creating a document that shows exactly which device occupies each rack unit, front and back, in a given cabinet. Which artifact is he producing?
feedback: A rack diagram (or rack elevation) shows unit-by-unit placement within a rack. Cable maps show point-to-point cabling; logical diagrams abstract away physical placement.

question: Which of the following are appropriate entries in an asset inventory?
correct: Hardware make, model, and serial number
Installed operating system and firmware versions
Software license counts and warranty expiration dates
incorrect: The wireless PSK shared with guest users
Every website visited from each workstation
feedback: Asset inventories track hardware, software, licensing, and warranty/support status. Secrets and user activity belong in other systems, not a general asset inventory.

question: Which system is used to centrally track the assignment and utilization of IPv4 and IPv6 addresses, subnets, DHCP scopes, and DNS records?
feedback: IPAM consolidates IP address inventory, subnet allocation, DHCP, and DNS into one authoritative system — eliminating spreadsheets that quickly drift out of date.

question: Silverkeep signs a contract with an ISP that guarantees 99.9% uptime and a four-hour mean time to repair on faults. This contractual commitment is documented in which artifact?
feedback: An SLA formally defines availability, performance, and response targets between a provider and a customer, typically with penalties for missing them.

question: Which deliverable from a wireless site survey shows signal coverage as colored bands overlaid on the floor plan so dead zones are easy to spot?
feedback: A heat map visualizes signal strength and coverage across a floor plan. It's the main output of a wireless site survey.

question: Which of the following best distinguishes end-of-life (EOL) from end-of-support (EOS) for a network device?
correct: EOL marks when the vendor stops selling the product; EOS marks when the vendor stops providing updates and support
incorrect: EOL and EOS are synonyms for the same milestone
EOL means the device is physically broken, while EOS means it is still new
EOS happens before EOL for every product
feedback: EOL is a sales milestone; EOS is a support milestone that usually follows later. Running EOS equipment is a security and compliance risk because patches are no longer issued.

question: Which of the following are good reasons to maintain a formal decommissioning procedure for retiring network equipment?
correct: To ensure configuration and customer data are wiped before disposal
To update asset inventory and IPAM records when the device is removed
To cancel support contracts and licensing for the retired hardware
incorrect: To make sure old equipment is sold without wiping configuration
To keep the device's management credentials published on the company wiki
To leave the device cabled and powered so it continues to appear in monitoring
feedback: Decommissioning ensures data is wiped, records are updated, contracts are ended, and the device is physically removed — reducing risk, cost, and noise in monitoring.

question: Which of the following is NOT typically part of a software and firmware management program?
correct: Disabling all updates indefinitely on production systems
incorrect: Applying security patches and bug fixes on a defined cadence
Keeping operating system versions within vendor-supported releases
Tracking firmware versions on switches, APs, and firewalls
feedback: Indefinitely disabling updates creates unsupported, insecure systems. A good program schedules patching, tests updates, and tracks versions across the fleet.

question: Which of the following describes the purpose of a formal change management process?
correct: To propose, review, approve, schedule, and document changes to production infrastructure
incorrect: To let any administrator deploy changes without review or tracking
To replace backup and recovery procedures
To store encryption keys and passwords
feedback: Change management exists to reduce the risk of outages caused by unreviewed changes and to provide a paper trail for audits and post-incident reviews.

question: Aria refers to a 'golden configuration' for her edge routers. What does that mean?
correct: A known-good baseline configuration that other devices are compared against and returned to
incorrect: The most recent configuration saved to the running-config, regardless of correctness
A backup of the configuration from when the device was first unboxed
A configuration stored only in the administrator's memory, never written down
feedback: A golden (baseline) configuration is the sanctioned reference. Production configs are compared against it to detect drift, and devices can be rebuilt from it during recovery.

question: The artifact that formally defines uptime, performance, and response-time commitments between a service provider and its customer is called a(n) ___.
feedback: The SLA is the contractual document behind the marketing '99.9% uptime' numbers. Missing the SLA typically triggers credits or penalties.

question: The practice of maintaining a central, authoritative inventory of IP addresses, subnets, and DNS/DHCP records is abbreviated as ___.
feedback: IPAM (IP Address Management) replaces the ubiquitous 'IP spreadsheet' with an authoritative database that tracks assignments and often integrates with DHCP and DNS.

question: Match each documentation artifact to what it primarily records.

pairs.definition: The actual cabling and physical placement of devices
IP addressing, VLANs, and routing relationships, abstracted from cabling
Unit-by-unit device placement within equipment racks
Signal coverage overlaid on a building floor plan
Hardware, software, licensing, and warranty information for each device

feedback: Each artifact answers a different question: 'what is plugged where', 'how does traffic flow logically', 'what is in this rack', 'where is the Wi-Fi weak', and 'what do we own'.

question: Which protocol is most commonly used to poll network devices for interface counters, CPU, memory, and other operational statistics?
feedback: SNMP is the classic poll/query protocol for device monitoring. Syslog carries event/log messages; NetFlow carries per-flow traffic data.

question: Which of the following are accurate comparisons of SNMPv2c and SNMPv3?
correct: SNMPv2c uses community strings that are sent in cleartext over the network
SNMPv3 adds authentication and optional encryption of SNMP messages
SNMPv3 is strongly preferred when monitoring devices over untrusted networks
incorrect: SNMPv2c encrypts all traffic by default
SNMPv3 has been deprecated in favor of SNMPv2c
SNMPv2c and SNMPv3 use identical authentication mechanisms
feedback: SNMPv2c is simple but insecure. SNMPv3 adds user-based authentication and optional privacy (encryption). Modern networks should use v3 wherever possible.

question: An SNMP agent sends an unsolicited notification to its management station when a specific condition (such as an interface going down) occurs. What is this notification called?
feedback: Traps (and informs) are unsolicited SNMP notifications from an agent to a manager. Polls go the other way — the manager asking the agent for data.

question: The hierarchical database of objects (OIDs) that an SNMP agent can be queried for is called the ___.
feedback: Each SNMP-capable device exposes a MIB — a structured tree of Object Identifiers representing counters, status values, and settings that can be read or (sometimes) written.

question: Brom is troubleshooting an application slowness complaint and wants to see every packet exchanged between a specific server and client. Which technique does he use?
correct: Full packet capture with a tool like Wireshark
incorrect: Polling the device via SNMP for interface counters
feedback: Packet capture is the deepest level of inspection, showing every header and payload. SNMP and syslog give higher-level summaries; traceroute only shows path, not content.

question: Which feature on a switch copies traffic from one or more source ports to a destination port where a capture device is attached?
feedback: Port mirroring (also called SPAN on Cisco gear) duplicates traffic to a monitoring port so an analyzer or IDS can inspect it without sitting inline.

question: Which technology summarizes traffic as per-flow records (source, destination, ports, bytes, packets) rather than capturing every individual packet?
feedback: Flow data (NetFlow, IPFIX, sFlow) scales much better than full packet capture for traffic analysis. It answers 'who talked to whom' rather than 'exactly what was said'.

question: Which of the following are true about baseline metrics in network monitoring?
correct: They establish what 'normal' utilization and performance look like for a given network
They are used to detect anomalies such as unusual spikes or sustained deviations
Anomaly-based alerting typically compares current values against the baseline
incorrect: A baseline only needs to be measured once and never updated
Baselines replace the need for any alerting thresholds
Baselines are only meaningful on wireless networks
feedback: Baselines capture normal behavior so that anomalies stand out. They need periodic refresh as the network evolves.

question: Zinna configures every device — switches, routers, firewalls, and servers — to forward their log messages to a central collector. Which protocol and tool category is she using?
correct: Syslog messages sent to a central syslog collector / SIEM
incorrect: NetFlow export to a flow collector
Port mirroring to a packet capture host
feedback: Syslog is the de facto protocol for event logs from network gear. A SIEM aggregates, normalizes, and correlates those logs (plus other telemetry) for security and operations.

question: Which monitoring solution category primarily answers the question 'is this device or service reachable right now'?
feedback: Availability monitoring (pings, health checks, service probes) answers 'is it up'. Performance monitoring answers 'how fast'. Configuration monitoring answers 'is the config as expected'.

question: Which solution continuously compares a device's running configuration against a baseline and alerts when unauthorized drift is detected?
feedback: Configuration monitoring/compliance tools flag drift from the golden configuration — a common source of outages and security gaps.

question: Which of the following are true about network discovery?
correct: Ad hoc discovery is run on demand to quickly enumerate hosts or services
Scheduled discovery runs on a recurring basis to keep inventories up to date
Discovery tools often use ICMP, ARP, and SNMP to identify devices
incorrect: Discovery can only be done manually by visiting each device in person
Scheduled discovery is only useful on wireless networks
Ad hoc discovery requires full packet capture of all traffic
feedback: Discovery — ad hoc or scheduled — keeps the authoritative view of the network fresh. It's how IPAM, CMDBs, and security tools learn what exists.

question: Mira's team wants their monitoring system to pull alarms and incidents from the firewall vendor's cloud portal automatically instead of scraping a web dashboard. Which integration approach is most appropriate?
correct: API integration with the vendor's REST API
incorrect: Manual copy and paste from the vendor's web UI
Port mirroring the firewall's WAN interface
Installing SNMPv1 community strings on the firewall
feedback: Modern monitoring and ticketing systems integrate with each other via APIs, typically REST. This is far more reliable than UI scraping or manual data entry.

question: A centralized platform that collects, normalizes, and correlates log data from many sources for security analysis is called a ___.
feedback: A SIEM aggregates and correlates log data to surface incidents that would be hidden in any single source. Its effectiveness depends on getting broad, clean log feeds.

question: Match each monitoring method to its primary use.

pairs.definition: Polling devices for counters and operational metrics, or receiving traps
Summarizing traffic as per-flow records for traffic analysis
Forwarding event and log messages to a central collector
Recording full packet contents for deep protocol-level inspection
Copying traffic from production ports to a monitoring port for analysis

feedback: These methods operate at different levels of detail and cost. A mature monitoring stack combines all of them.
